title: Address sanitizer 22.04 fails on verilated_trace_imp.h
body:
Changing ci/ci-script.bash to have the sanitizer run on 22.04 instead of 20.04 gives the below.  No other tests fail.

@gezalore think you were last in there, would you mind looking?  I'm trying to move regressions to 22.04, but there is no rush in that.

```
======================================================================
vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst: ==================================================
/verilator/test_regress/../include/gtkwave/fstapi.c:858:22: runtime error: store to misaligned address 0x7f5ae938f801 for type 'uint32_t', which requires 4 byte alignment
0x7f5ae938f801: note: pointer points here
 00 00 00  21 be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be
              ^
/verilator/test_regress/../include/gtkwave/fstapi.c:1366:51: runtime error: load of misaligned address 0x7f5ae938f801 for type 'uint32_t', which requires 4 byte alignment
0x7f5ae938f801: note: pointer points here
 00 00 00  21 00 00 00 00 00 78 00  00 00 00 00 78 78 78 78  78 78 78 78 78 78 78 78  78 78 78 78 78
              ^
/verilator/test_regress/../include/gtkwave/fstapi.c:1431:43: runtime error: load of misaligned address 0x7f5ae938f807 for type 'uint32_t', which requires 4 byte alignment
0x7f5ae938f807: note: pointer points here
 00 00 00 78 00  00 00 00 00 78 78 78 78  78 78 78 78 78 78 78 78  78 78 78 78 78 78 78 78  78 78 78
             ^
/verilator/test_regress/../include/gtkwave/fstapi.c:1663:116: runtime error: left shift of negative value -2
/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_trace_imp.h:603:69: runtime error: store to misaligned address 0x60d000000114 for type 'uint64_t', which requires 8 byte alignment
0x60d000000114: note: pointer points here
  0c 00 00 00 be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be  be be be be be be be be
              ^
/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_trace_imp.h:195:31: runtime error: load of misaligned address 0x60d000000114 for type 'const uint64_t', which requires 8 byte alignment
0x60d000000114: note: pointer points here
  0c 00 00 00 01 00 00 00  00 00 00 00 0d 00 00 00  30 03 00 00 70 60 00 00  00 00 00 00 02 00 00 00
              ^
=================================================================

WRITE of size 4 at 0x60d0000002e0 thread T0
    #0 0x5575b4eb3930 in VerilatedTraceOffloadBuffer<VerilatedFstBuffer>::VerilatedTraceOffloadBuffer(VerilatedFst&) (/verilator/test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/Vt_trace_cat_fst+0x363930)
    #1 0x5575b4ebbfe5 in VerilatedFst::getTraceBuffer() (/verilator/test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/Vt_trace_cat_fst+0x36bfe5)
    #2 0x5575b4ea4eee in VerilatedTrace<VerilatedFst, VerilatedFstBuffer>::runOffloadedCallbacks(std::vector<VerilatedTrace<VerilatedFst, VerilatedFstBuffer>::CallbackRecord, std::allocator<Veril$
    #3 0x5575b4ea7a48 in VerilatedTrace<VerilatedFst, VerilatedFstBuffer>::dump(unsigned long) (/verilator/test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/Vt_trace_cat_fst+0x357a48)

previously allocated by thread T0 here:
    #0 0x7f5b08247337 in operator new[](unsigned long) ../../../../src/libsanitizer/asan/asan_new_delete.cpp:102

    #5 0x5575b4d99e55 in main (/verilator/test_regress/obj_vltmt/t_trace_cat_fst/Vt_trace_cat_fst+0x249e55)
```
